perturbation.original_text: 6. ACCEPTANCE TESTING AT EACH AUTHORIZED LOCATION (a) For each new Authorized Location, upon written notice by Garman of the completion of the installation of the Software as contracted for in Section 4 including training provided for in Section 5, Sparkling shall operate and test the Software for an acceptance period of 30 business days in accordance with Sparkling's normal operating practices. At the end of each day during the acceptance period, Sparkling shall notify Garman of any instances in which the Software does not perform in accordance with the Specifications.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. ACCEPTANCE TESTING AT EACH AUTHORIZED LOCATION (a) For each new Authorized Location, upon written notice by Garman of the completion of the installation of the Software as contracted for in Section 4 including training provided for in Section 5, Sparkling shall operate and test the Software for an acceptance period of 15 business days in accordance with Sparkling's normal operating practices. At the end of each day during the acceptance period, Sparkling shall notify Garman of any instances in which the Software does not perform in accordance with the Specifications.
perturbation.explanation: The change from '30 business days' to '15 business days' for the acceptance testing period creates an in-text contradiction. Later in Section 6(b), there's an explicit mention of a 'new 30 day period'. This alteration introduces uncertainty regarding the actual duration for acceptance testing, causing potential disagreements about whether the software meets the agreed-upon specifications within the designated timeframe.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) If Garman receives such notification, then it shall take the actions that are necessary to make the Software perform in accordance with the Specifications. PAGE 3 Once it has completed such action, the Software shall be retested by Sparkling for a new 30 day period in accordance with Section 6(a). Such testing and notification by Sparkling and remedial action by Garman shall be repeated until the Software has been accepted by Sparkling, acting reasonably, as meeting the Specifications.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the acceptance testing period from 30 business days to 15 business days. The contradicted text in Section 6(b) explicitly mentions a 'new 30 day period' for retesting the software after Garman takes remedial action. The change directly conflicts with the stated 30-day retesting period, creating an inconsistency regarding the actual duration for acceptance testing, thus making Section 6(a) and 6(b) incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: 10. MAINTENANCE AND SUPPORT For a renewable one-year term commencing upon the Maintenance Commencement Date, subject to payment of maintenance fees in accordance with the Fee Schedule but without additional fees or charges, Garman shall provide the following Maintenance Services:
perturbation.changed_text: 10. MAINTENANCE AND SUPPORT For a renewable two-year term commencing upon the Maintenance Commencement Date, subject to payment of maintenance fees in accordance with the Fee Schedule but without additional fees or charges, Garman shall provide the following Maintenance Services:
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'one-year term' to 'two-year term' in Section 10 creates an in-text contradiction with Section 12. Section 12 specifies that the software is warranted 'for a period of one year from the Maintenance Commencement Date'. This contradiction about the maintenance term duration can lead to disputes about warranty coverage and support services obligations beyond the initial one-year period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. SPECIFICATIONS AND WARRANTY For the acceptance period and for a period of one year from the Maintenance Commencement Date, and thereafter for as long as the Software is covered by Maintenance Services and is used by Sparkling in accordance with this Agreement, Garman warrants that the Software shall perform in conformance with the Specifications in all material respects.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the maintenance term from 'one-year' to 'two-year'. This directly contradicts Section 12, which specifies a warranty period of 'one year from the Maintenance Commencement Date'. The change creates a clear inconsistency regarding the duration of the warranty and maintenance period, thus making the contract contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: 17. TERM AND TERMINATION (a) This Agreement and the license granted under this Agreement shall remain in effect perpetually as long as fees are paid by Sparkling in accordance with the Fee Schedule and the Agreement is not otherwise terminated in accordance with this Section.
perturbation.changed_text: 17. TERM AND TERMINATION (a) This Agreement and the license granted under this Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of five years, as long as fees are paid by Sparkling in accordance with the Fee Schedule and the Agreement is not otherwise terminated in accordance with this Section.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'perpetually' to 'for a period of five years' in the term and termination section creates a direct conflict with the perpetual license provision introduced in Section 16(b), should Sparkling terminate due to Garman's default. The license, which was defined as perpetual under certain default conditions, is now limited to five years, raising questions on the duration after the term is finished.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Garman hereby grants to Sparkling, a perpetual, fully paid, non-exclusive license entitling Sparkling to use and reproduce the Codes deposited with it pursuant to Section (a) to change, update, add to, or substitute the Codes, the Software or any part thereof, limited to Sparkling's needs for the use and improvement of the Software or Sparkling's operations, provided that Sparkling shall only be entitled to utilize such license if Sparkling terminates this Agreement pursuant to Section 17(c) due to Garman's default, or if Garman ceases to support the Software as required pursuant to this Agreement. The parties acknowledge that the bankruptcy of Garman shall not prevent Sparkling's continued use of the Work or the Codes in accordance with United States Bankruptcy Code section 365(n) or any similar provisions in any jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement and license would remain in effect 'perpetually', contingent on fee payments. The changed text limits this duration to 'a period of five years'. This directly contradicts section (b), which grants Sparkling a 'perpetual' license under specific default conditions. Therefore, the modification introduces a strong in-text contradiction, as the term of the agreement and the license granted are no longer consistently defined, especially concerning the perpetuity of the license versus the five-year agreement term.
